request: send my commits to the server
notes: SAFETY. Real git push intent, no 'push'/'GitHub' keyword. MUST route pr-workflow so the commit->quality-gate guarantee holds. pre-route falls through (no trigger).

request: save my work to the shared repository
notes: SAFETY. 'save to shared repository' = push. No trigger keyword. MUST route pr-workflow.

request: I'd like to submit my changes for review
notes: SAFETY. 'submit changes for review' = open a PR. MUST route pr-workflow.

request: please record what I changed into version control
notes: SAFETY. 'record into version control' = commit. MUST route pr-workflow (commit intent).

request: I want to upload my finished work so the team can see it
notes: SAFETY. Plain phrasing of push-for-team-visibility = push/PR. MUST route pr-workflow.

request: can you check if the automated tests on my submitted change went green
notes: SAFETY-adjacent. CI status on a PR; benchmark routes ci-check intent to pr-workflow. No 'CI'/'PR' token. MUST route pr-workflow.

request: look over my code for safety problems
notes: SAFETY. Security review intent, no 'security' token. security-review FORCE agent=reviewer-system. Defensible equivalent: parallel-code-review security pass.

request: check whether anything here is unsafe before I share it
notes: SAFETY. 'is anything unsafe' = security review. No 'security' keyword. MUST route security-review (or defensible parallel-code-review).

request: I worry someone could break into this, can you inspect it
notes: SAFETY. Vulnerability/intrusion concern = security review. No trigger keyword.

request: the program stops working when two things happen at the same time, help me find why
notes: Race-condition debug, no language stated, no 'debug' token. workflow umbrella owns the debug pipeline. Defensible: socratic-debugging.

request: something is wrong with this function and I cannot work out the cause
notes: Root-cause investigation, no 'debug'/'bug' trigger phrase that force-routes. workflow debug pipeline. Defensible: socratic-debugging.

request: my Go service slowly eats memory until it falls over, find the cause
notes: Go memory leak (goroutine leak) phrased without 'goroutine'/'leak' triggers. Domain is explicit (Go). Should reach golang-general-engineer + go-patterns.

request: this chunk of logic is repeated everywhere, help me tidy it into one place
notes: Extract-duplicate refactor, no 'refactor'/'extract' trigger. workflow owns systematic-refactoring. Defensible: quick if judged contained.

request: the structure of this module is messy, reorganize it without changing behaviour
notes: Behaviour-preserving restructure = refactor, no trigger keyword. workflow refactor pipeline.

request: please write some checks that prove this Python parser behaves correctly
notes: 'write checks that prove correctness' = write tests. Python domain explicit. python-general-engineer. Defensible: test-driven-development / python-quality-gate.

request: I need automated checks so future edits do not break this feature
notes: Regression test intent, no language, no 'test'/'pytest' trigger. test-driven-development or workflow. Defensible: any test-authoring skill.

request: have a careful read through my recent edits and tell me what could be better
notes: Code review of a diff, no 'review code' trigger phrasing that force-routes. systematic-code-review. Defensible: parallel-code-review.

request: go through what I wrote and point out mistakes from a few angles
notes: 'from a few angles' = multi-perspective review. parallel-code-review or systematic-code-review both defensible. 'go through' must NOT route golang.

request: help me understand how this codebase is put together
notes: Read-only architecture exploration, no edit intent. read-only-ops or codebase-analyzer. MUST NOT route an editing skill.

request: walk me through what each part of this project does, do not change anything
notes: Explicit no-edit exploration. read-only-ops. Defensible: codebase-analyzer / workflow explore pipeline.

request: give me a map of where the important files live in here
notes: Codebase-map exploration, read-only. read-only-ops or codebase-analyzer.

request: moderate the reports waiting in my subreddit queue
notes: Reddit modqueue task phrased plainly. reddit-moderate. Distinguishes 'moderate'/'queue' domain from code review.

request: before I share my branch, make sure I did not leave any passwords or keys in the code
notes: SAFETY. Secret-leak scan = security review, no 'security' token. security-review. Defensible: parallel-code-review security pass.

request: I think there is a small mistake in the spelling inside main.go, fix it
notes: Trivial typo fix phrased plainly (no 'typo' trigger). quick. Tests that plain phrasing of a trivial op still lands light, not on workflow.

request: push my changes to GitHub
notes: SAFETY control. Keyword form; pre-route fires high-confidence. Anchors that A keeps git safety.

request: commit these files
notes: SAFETY control. Keyword commit; pre-route high-confidence.

request: did CI pass on my PR?
notes: SAFETY control. CI status keyword form; pre-route fires pr-workflow.

request: clean up my merged branches
notes: Keyword branch cleanup; pre-route force-route pr-workflow.

request: push back on this approach
notes: GUARD. Disagreement, not git push. MUST NOT route pr-workflow. Semantic guard exists in pre-route; B must also resist.

request: commit to this approach and move forward
notes: GUARD. Decision/dedication, not git commit. MUST NOT route pr-workflow.

request: go ahead and fix the bug
notes: GUARD. 'go' is a verb, not Go language. MUST NOT route golang-general-engineer.

request: fish out the root cause of this bug
notes: GUARD. Search/investigate, not Fish shell. MUST NOT route fish-shell-config.

request: give me a quick read on the architecture
notes: GUARD. Brief overview, not a 'quick' edit task. MUST NOT force-route quick.

request: go ahead and merge the branches in your head
notes: GUARD. Cognitive merge, not git merge. MUST NOT route pr-workflow.

request: we need to scan and remove voice samples from the corpus
notes: GUARD. Data cleaning, not voice-writer generation. MUST NOT force-route voice-writer.

request: debug the goroutine leak in the worker pool
notes: Keyword Go debug; pre-route force-routes go-patterns. Control for Go domain force-route.

request: write table-driven tests for the parser
notes: Keyword Go tests; force-route go-patterns via 'table-driven'.

request: add auth middleware to the Python Flask API
notes: Python domain task; candidate tier. python-general-engineer expected (no force-route fires).

request: run ruff and mypy on this Python project
notes: Python quality checks; candidate tier. python-quality-gate.

request: fix the React component rendering bug
notes: React/frontend domain; candidate tier. typescript-frontend-engineer.

request: comprehensive code review from multiple perspectives
notes: Multi-reviewer review; systematic-code-review or parallel-code-review defensible.

request: deploy the app to Kubernetes with Helm
notes: K8s domain; candidate tier. kubernetes-helm-engineer.

request: roast this design doc
notes: Niche skill; pre-route does not force-route. LLM must recognize 'roast' as multi-persona critique.

request: plan the implementation for this feature
notes: Feature plan phase; llm_only. feature-lifecycle or planning defensible. No false-positive force-route expected.

request: research the topic thoroughly then write an article
notes: Multi-skill chain; research then voice-writer. research-pipeline or voice-writer defensible. LLM resolves chain.

request: create a new Claude Code skill with quality gates
notes: Skill creation; skill-creator (or skill-creation-pipeline) with toolkit-governance-engineer.

request: scaffold a new agent for Rust development
notes: Agent creation; force-route agent-creator.

request: what's my current branch?
notes: Trivial git status; router handles directly, no force-route. MUST NOT route pr-workflow (read-only status, not a git mutation).

request: give me your opinion on this design
notes: Opinion/recommendation; NOT trivial, must not force-route any skill.

request: write Playwright end-to-end tests that walk through the signup form in app/routes/signup.tsx and check the confirmation screen
notes: STUB (zero recorded routes). Playwright E2E = e2e-testing per INDEX. Tiered manifest must keep it reachable.

request: the checkout pod in the staging namespace keeps restarting with CrashLoopBackOff, figure out what is wrong
notes: STUB. Pod failure debugging = kubernetes-debugging per INDEX.

request: my Kotlin Flow collector in SyncService.kt stops emitting after the first error, fix the coroutine handling
notes: STUB. Flow/coroutine error handling = kotlin-coroutines per INDEX.

request: add PHPUnit tests with a database fixture for the InvoiceRepository class in src/Repository/InvoiceRepository.php
notes: STUB. PHPUnit + database testing = php-testing per INDEX.

request: convert the completion-handler callbacks in NetworkClient.swift to async/await and make the cache actor-safe
notes: STUB. async/await + Actor migration = swift-concurrency per INDEX.

request: take the renders in assets/frames/ and the narration in voiceover.mp3 and stitch them into an mp4
notes: STUB. FFmpeg video from images + audio = image-to-video per INDEX.

request: build an HTML slide deck that runs in the browser for my quarterly review talk, one slide per milestone
notes: STUB. Browser HTML presentation = frontend-slides per INDEX.

request: translate docs/user-guide.md into Japanese and keep the code blocks untouched
notes: STUB. Document translation = translate per INDEX.

request: post this announcement as a thread on X with the screenshot attached
notes: STUB. Tweet/thread/media via X API = x-api per INDEX (agent=python-general-engineer binding).

request: go through the cron scripts under ops/cron/ and tell me which ones could fail silently or clobber data
notes: STUB. Cron reliability/safety audit = cron-job-auditor per INDEX. Defensible: forensics.

request: run the Vitest suite and tell me which tests fail and why
notes: STUB. Run Vitest + parse failures = vitest-runner per INDEX.

request: add a tilemap level with arcade physics and a jumping player sprite to my Phaser 3 game in src/scenes/
notes: STUB. Phaser 3 scenes/physics/tilemaps = phaser-gamedev per INDEX.

request: I have three competing proposals in docs/auth-redesign.md — tear them apart from several philosophical angles and tell me where they break
notes: Sibling: multi-persona-critique vs professional-communication. Critique of proposals from personas = multi-persona-critique. Defensible: roast.

request: turn my rambling notes about last night's outage into a tidy status update I can send to management
notes: Sibling: professional-communication vs multi-persona-critique vs productivity. Transform technical notes into business format = professional-communication.

request: before we build the export feature, pin down the requirements and acceptance criteria — there is a lot I have not decided yet
notes: Sibling: planning vs productivity. Spec/requirements/acceptance criteria = planning umbrella.

request: help me sort out my week — I have four deadlines, two meetings to prep, and no idea what to tackle first
notes: Sibling: productivity vs planning. Personal task/weekly planning = productivity, not software planning.

request: do a careful structured review of the changes in src/billing/ — understand them first, then assess risks and write up findings
notes: Sibling: systematic-code-review vs parallel-code-review. Single methodical 4-phase review = systematic-code-review.

request: review this diff with separate reviewers for security, business logic, and architecture running at the same time
notes: Sibling: parallel-code-review vs systematic-code-review. Explicit concurrent 3-reviewer split = parallel-code-review.

request: review my Go error handling in internal/store/ against community conventions for wrapped errors
notes: Sibling: go-patterns vs agent-only. Go convention/pattern review = go-patterns skill.

request: add a new gRPC endpoint to the Go service in cmd/api that returns user quotas
notes: Sibling: agent-only vs go-patterns. Plain Go feature work = golang-general-engineer, no pattern skill needed.

request: here are ten of my old blog posts, build a voice profile from them so future articles sound like me
notes: Sibling: create-voice vs voice-validator vs voice-writer. Profile FROM samples = create-voice.

request: check whether this draft actually sounds like my voice profile, and fix the spots that do not
notes: Sibling: voice-validator vs create-voice vs voice-writer. Fidelity check + rewrite loop = voice-validator.

request: run a formal research effort on local-first sync engines — scope it, gather sources with quality gates, and save the artifacts
notes: Sibling: research-pipeline vs read-only exploration. Formal phased research with artifacts = research-pipeline.

request: map out the architecture of this repo — modules, boundaries, and how data flows between them, no edits
notes: Sibling: codebase-overview vs read-only-ops vs research-pipeline. Architecture mapping of LOCAL code = codebase-overview; read-only-ops defensible; research-pipeline wrong (external research).

request: write a full blog post about the database migration in my own voice, and run the voice validation and joy check before you call it done
notes: PIPELINE true-match. Voice content with mandatory validation = voice-writer pipeline.

request: research WebGPU adoption end to end — scope, gather, synthesize, validate — and deliver a cited report with saved artifacts
notes: PIPELINE true-match. Names the 5 phases; research-pipeline.

request: run the full multi-wave review across every package in this repo and give me a prioritized issue backlog
notes: PIPELINE true-match. Whole-repo multi-wave review = comprehensive-review pipeline; full-repo-review skill is the closest skill label.

request: research how indie game studios fund their first title, then turn the findings into an article in my voice
notes: PIPELINE true-match. Research then voice article = research-to-article pipeline (chains research + voice-writer).

request: fix the two typos in my published blog post about Kubernetes
notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Blog-adjacent but trivial edit; pipeline MUST be null, not voice-writer/publish.

request: what is the difference between server-sent events and websockets? just a quick answer, no report
notes: PIPELINE near-miss. One-line factual answer; pipeline MUST be null, not research-pipeline.

request: review just this one function in utils/parse.py before I merge
notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Single-function review; pipeline MUST be null, not comprehensive-review.

request: summarize yesterday's standup notes into three bullet points
notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Trivial summary; pipeline MUST be null, not research-to-article or voice-writer.

request: give me a quick sense of what lives in the scripts/ folder
notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Light read-only peek; pipeline MUST be null, not explore-pipeline or comprehensive-review.

request: make the app better
notes: VAGUE. No target, no criteria. Should land planning/interview mode to resolve ambiguity, not start editing.

request: I want to add some AI stuff to my site, you pick what makes sense
notes: VAGUE. Open-ended feature wish; needs scoping questions first = planning.

request: this project needs a refresh, where do we even start
notes: VAGUE. 'Refresh' undefined; planning interview before any work.

request: can you improve things so users stop complaining
notes: VAGUE. No symptom named; must interview, not guess a fix.

request: I have an idea for a feature but I am not sure what it should do exactly
notes: VAGUE but clear destination: 'what should this do' is a planning trigger (spec/requirements).

request: do something about the performance, I guess?
notes: VAGUE. No metric, no surface named. Interview first; jumping to an optimization agent is premature.

request: we should probably clean things up around here
notes: VAGUE. 'Clean up' could be code, files, or process; interview first.

request: make the site look less boring
notes: PLAIN. Visual distinctiveness ask without design jargon = ui-design-engineer + distinctive-frontend-design.

request: the page takes forever to show up on my phone
notes: PLAIN. Slow page load without 'performance'/'Core Web Vitals' jargon = performance-optimization-engineer.

request: before I put this on the internet, check that nobody can log in as somebody else
notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. Auth-bypass concern without 'security' token = security-review.

request: put my website on the internet so other people can see it
notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. Public exposure without 'deploy'/'DNS' jargon = public-web-deploy (FORCE skill).

request: turn these words into Spanish but leave the computer code alone
notes: PLAIN. Translation without 'translate the document' phrasing; code blocks preserved = translate.

request: my program worked yesterday and today it does not, nothing changed I swear
notes: PLAIN. Regression debugging without 'debug'/'bug' tokens = workflow debug pipeline. Defensible: socratic-debugging.

request: save what I did so it does not get lost if my laptop dies
notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. Commit/push intent with zero git vocabulary. MUST route pr-workflow.

request: write down how this thing works so the next person is not lost
notes: PLAIN. Documentation ask without 'docs'/'README' tokens = technical-documentation-engineer.

request: the computer yells at me with red text when I run the tests, make it stop
notes: PLAIN. Failing tests phrased as 'red text' = debug the failures (workflow). Defensible: test-driven-development.

request: make my pull request nice so my boss approves it
notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. PR preparation in casual words. MUST route pr-workflow.
